title: lathyrus articulatus subsp. euclymenum
intro: Lathyrus articulatus subsp. euclymenum, also known as a variety of the sweet pea, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family. It is a perennial legume with medicinal properties, particularly in its seeds, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The seeds of Lathyrus articulatus subsp. euclymenum are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer, and also provide relief from symptoms of menopause, arthritis, and other conditions. The seeds can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, and may also be added to food products or used as a dietary supplement.
